_comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the viewing-spot pins. Edit this file to add/remove spots. Every location here is INSIDE the path of totality (verified against the eclipse's Besselian elements). Durations/times/altitude are NOT stored — they are computed live in the browser from js/eclipse.js. Votes and user-suggested spots live in the visitor's browser (localStorage); a shared cross-visitor database needs a backend.

spots.name: Wyndham
spots.desc: Australia's northernmost town and the eclipse's mainland landfall — the umbra crosses the Kimberley here first, with totality high over the Cambridge Gulf.

spots.name: Kununurra
spots.desc: Gateway to the Bungle Bungles and Lake Argyle, with vast open horizons. Near the northern edge of the path — nudge south toward the central line for longer totality.

spots.name: Tennant Creek
spots.desc: Goldmining town on the Stuart Highway, close to the central line — over three minutes of totality in reliably clear desert air.

spots.name: Devils Marbles / Karlu Karlu
spots.desc: Sacred boulder field almost exactly on the central line — nearly five minutes of totality, the longest of any easily named landmark on the path. The black Sun above red spheroidal granite will be one of the most photographed frames on Earth that day.
spots.weather: Dry winter desert air — excellent odds.

spots.name: Hungerford — Eclipse Crossroads
spots.desc: The spot where the paths of the 2028 and 2030 total eclipses cross. Stand here for 4m 15s of totality in July 2028 — then come back on 25 Nov 2030 for a second totality at sunset. Paroo River country beside Currawinya National Park, near the tiny border town of Hungerford.

spots.name: Dubbo
spots.desc: Rural hub squarely inside the path, with Taronga Western Plains Zoo and the dark skies of Warrumbungle National Park nearby.
spots.weather: Higher cloud risk than the outback — ~55% clear.

spots.name: Bathurst
spots.desc: Historic gold-country town west of the Blue Mountains — three minutes of totality over the tablelands.
spots.weather: Cold and often clearer than the coast in winter.

spots.name: Blue Mountains (Katoomba)
spots.desc: Sandstone escarpment above the Jamison Valley — the Three Sisters and Echo Point promise the eclipsed Sun framed by ancient forest.

spots.name: Sydney Harbour
spots.desc: The first totality over Sydney since 1857. The Royal Botanic Garden sits squarely in the path — the corona hanging above the Harbour Bridge and Opera House in the early afternoon.

spots.name: Wollongong
spots.desc: Coastal city where the path meets the Tasman — totality over the sea cliffs and lighthouses of the Illawarra.
spots.weather: Coastal winter cloud is a gamble.

spots.name: Milford Sound
spots.desc: One of the world's most dramatic fjords — totality with the Sun low over Mitre Peak in late-winter light. A bucket-list frame if the skies cooperate.
spots.weather: Fiordland is one of Earth's wettest places. High risk.

spots.name: Queenstown
spots.desc: Alpine resort on Lake Wakatipu — a low winter Sun over the Remarkables during totality.
spots.weather: Winter cloud is significant; a mountain vantage helps.

spots.name: Dunedin
spots.desc: Coastal Otago city — the final major landfall of totality before the shadow races into the South Pacific.
